chunks.speaker: interviewer
chunks.raw_text: Well, let me give you a high level overview of what our product does. And then when that day comes, you'll be prepared for it. And we hope you do. But basically, what we're building is an AI system that plans your week. And it reprioritizes when fire drills hit. So you can stay focused on what matters most. So some of the ways that we're doing that is we're finding a lot of people, because they're working with clients, are living out of a lot of systems. They're living out of their calendars, notion, motion. And all their clients are kind of doing things in different places. They might have three or four calendars. And so we're pulling all that work into one place. So you can just look at everything from one command center. And also, we're able to break that down by client. So that you could have a breakdown to see, okay, I have this much work with client A, client B, client C. But then aggregate it all in one view. So that if you are spreading yourself thin across a lot of clients, you could see how you're still managing your personal time against that. And all of this is also powered by AI. But it's also powered by your goals. Not everybody has the same goal. Some people want to work 20 hours a week. Some people want to work 80 hours a week. Some people care a lot about work-life balance. Or maybe they want to do something like run a marathon or whatever. And then other people just have pure financial goals. I need to get X amount of dollars, X amount of clients. We have a goals module so that as we're prioritizing your task and managing your weekly capacity, it's based off of what you actually care about. Not just like the AI overlord making decisions for you. And part of that is it also deprioritizes things. So you only have so much capacity in a week. So by understanding how much time you have within a week, we can understand, well, what are your priorities? Because you can't do everything. What are we going to move down the list? And maybe you want to run a marathon, but you've got a lot of client work to do this week. So you can't put five hours into running. And maybe that just falls down the list, right? Or maybe you just keep putting that off. And it's been three weeks and you got a marathon coming up in two months. Well, it's like, well, now it's going to bump up your priority list. Or you need to change your goals. So it's using like... The more you use it, the more it understands you. And the better that it can help you manage your time. And one of the big things we're trying to fix is really some of these feelings. But it's that constant sense of being behind. And our hypothesis is the reason it happens is people don't actually measure what they're doing. And they don't go into their weeks against their actual capacity. And then what they do is they sacrifice their nights, their weekends, their free time to make up for it. And they just live in this constant state of feeling behind. And whatever gets tracked gets improved. And so we're providing a system to track it, but also avoid setting yourself up for failure. And like in the middle of the week, when you get in a fender bender or something happens with your kids, and now all of a sudden you're going to the doctor for four hours. Our job is to give you that peace of mind that it's okay. We could figure it out and kind of sort things out for you.
